file_name: BOLIVARMININGCORP_05_23_2003-EX-2.1-VISP WEB SITE BUILDING AND HOSTING AGREEMENT.txt

perturbation.original_text: This agreement shall renew automatically each year thereafter, unless either party serves written notice of its intention not to renew, on the other at least 90 days prior to the expiration of the then current term of this agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: This agreement shall renew automatically each year thereafter, unless either party serves written notice of its intention not to renew, on the other at least 30 days prior to the expiration of the then current term of this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the notification period for non-renewal from 90 days to 30 days creates a direct contradiction with the original agreement where the notification period is defined differently. This creates ambiguity about the actual term and the process for non-renewal.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This agreement shall be for a term of five years from the date of this document. This agreement shall renew automatically each year thereafter, unless either party serves written notice of its intention not to renew, on the other at least 30 days prior to the expiration of the then current term of this agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the notification period for non-renewal from 90 days to 30 days. The contradicted text states: 'This agreement shall renew automatically each year thereafter, unless either party serves written notice of its intention not to renew, on the other at least 30 days prior to the expiration of the then current term of this agreement.' The original text said 90 days. This is a direct, impactful, and clear contradiction, creating ambiguity about the actual required notification period for non-renewal. This creates ambiguity and would lead to disagreements between parties. This ambiguity directly impedes contract enforceability.

perturbation.original_text: PROVIDER'S RESPONSIBILITIES Provider agrees to provide KCI with VISP Private labeled website building and maintenance capabilities. Deliverables shall include: Sales collateral materials including brochures and flyers to help KCI sign customers to the service. A fully staffed back end including; Billing, real time credit card transactions, administrative reports, Customer and technical support, Online Customer Account Maintenance, Private labeled E-Mail Server, Administrative E-Mail Addresses, vacation e-mail, Web based and POP Access, Online Interfaces, a private labeled Internet portal for KCI and include an affiliate program. KCI's Customers will receive an initial VISP web site setup including 5Mbs of storage space, maintenance of the site, technical support, and any services necessary for use of the site for no charge.
perturbation.changed_text: PROVIDER'S RESPONSIBILITIES Provider agrees to provide KCI with VISP Private labeled website building and maintenance capabilities. Deliverables shall include: Sales collateral materials including brochures and flyers to help KCI sign customers to the service. A fully staffed back end including; Billing, real time credit card transactions, administrative reports, Customer and technical support, Online Customer Account Maintenance, Private labeled E-Mail Server, Administrative E-Mail Addresses, vacation e-mail, Web based and POP Access, Online Interfaces, a private labeled Internet portal for KCI and include an affiliate program. KCI's Customers may receive an initial VISP web site setup including 5Mbs of storage space, maintenance of the site, technical support, and any services necessary for use of the site for no charge.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'will receive' to 'may receive' introduces ambiguity regarding Provider's obligation to provide services to KCI's customers. This contradicts the initial agreement of providing services for KCI's customers for no charge, creating a significant in-text contradiction about the certainty of service provision.
perturbation.contradicted_text: PROVIDER'S RESPONSIBILITIES Provider agrees to provide KCI with VISP Private labeled website building and maintenance capabilities. Deliverables shall include: Sales collateral materials including brochures and flyers to help KCI sign customers to the service. A fully staffed back end including; Billing, real time credit card transactions, administrative reports, Customer and technical support, Online Customer Account Maintenance, Private labeled E-Mail Server, Administrative E-Mail Addresses, vacation e-mail, Web based and POP Access, Online Interfaces, a private labeled Internet portal for KCI and include an affiliate program. KCI's Customers may receive an initial VISP web site setup including 5Mbs of storage space, maintenance of the site, technical support, and any services necessary for use of the site for no charge.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'will receive' to 'may receive' introduces a significant ambiguity, creating a contradiction. The original text implies a guaranteed service provision to KCI's customers, whereas the changed text makes it optional. This directly contradicts the implicit obligation of providing services for KCI's customers for no charge, thus satisfying the definition of ambiguities leading to an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: KCI pledges that it will not circumvent the relationships among venders, providers and clients developed by Provider either directly or indirectly, during the contract period and for a period of up to 2 (two) years following termination of this contract
perturbation.changed_text: KCI pledges that it will not circumvent the relationships among venders, providers and clients developed by Provider either directly or indirectly, during the contract period and for a period of up to 1 (one) year following termination of this contract
perturbation.explanation: Changing the non-circumvention period from 2 years to 1 year following the termination of the contract introduces ambiguity as to how long the non-circumvention pledge is actually valid for. This contradicts the original agreement in the document where the term period of non-circumvention is defined differently, leading to uncertainty in enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: KCI pledges that it will not circumvent the relationships among venders, providers and clients developed by Provider either directly or indirectly, during the contract period and for a period of up to 1 (one) year following termination of this contract
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the non-circumvention period from 2 years to 1 year, while the contradicted text states it is 1 year. This creates an inconsistency within the contract regarding the duration of the non-circumvention obligation, which falls under Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction as the terms are defined inconsistently in different parts of a contract, making interpretation unclear.
